items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The council held a study session on Tuesday to examine planned infrastructure and development projects for the next fiscal year. This is where residents can learn what physical improvements—like roads, parks, or facilities—the city is prioritizing and funding.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The city is introducing an ordinance to amend its Residential Tenant Protections Program, which could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in Sunnyvale. Details on what specific protections are being added or modified were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: City releases library facilities and customer experience plan
items.summary: San José presented a status report on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, outlining improvements to library services and physical spaces across the system. This plan could affect where residents access library services and what amenities are available.

items.headline: Historic downtown building seeks density bonus for rehabilitation
items.summary: 405 Kipling Street, a Category 4 historic resource in downtown Palo Alto, is requesting elevation to Category 2 status and 5,000 additional square feet of floor area to support historic and seismic upgrades. The project would preserve a historic building while enabling additional development.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal to rezone and redevelop 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road would bring an 8-story building with 167 apartments (28 affordable units) and 1,400 sq ft of commercial space. This represents significant new housing and a land-use change from the current commercial zoning.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed to April 22
items.summary: A zoning decision for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been delayed and will be rescheduled for the April 22, 2026 hearing instead. Residents interested in this project should watch for updated notices with the new hearing date.

items.headline: City approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully clear from the agenda. Residents should monitor follow-up reports to understand what infrastructure or project this contract covers.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant agreement
items.summary: City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7. This project addresses traffic and rail crossing safety on one of San José's major corridors.
